name: Squash Soup with Cheese & Apples
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and seed the squashes. Cut enough into julienne to make 3/4 cup of each. Cover.
2. Cut the remaining squash into rough cubes. Chop the onion and celery.
3. In a large, heavy saucepan, heat the oil with 1-1/2 tablespoons water. Add the cubed squash, onion, and celery. Cover and cook over low heat, stirring occasionally about 10 minutes.
4. Make a medium roux of butter and flour and cook, stirring constantly for about five minutes.
5. Heat the stock and add to the roux, stirring briskly until smooth.
6. Combine the cooked vegetables with the thickened stock and bring to a simmer.
7. Lower the heat and cook, partly covered, until very tender (30 to 40 minutes).
8. Puree the soup. Grate the cheese. Peel and seed the apples and cut into thin slices.
9. Stir the julienne of squash into the soup and heat through for 3 minutes.
10. Remove from the heat and stir in the cheese and sliced apples. Season to taste.
